hypothesis: Training template-free retrosynthesis models with a class-balanced loss that upweights rare reaction templates proportional to the inverse square-root of their training frequency will increase top-10 accuracy on rare templates (frequency 1–10) by at least 25 percentage points and raise Pistachio OOD top-10 accuracy above 10% while preserving or improving top-5 round-trip accuracy on frequent templates (>10,000 occurrences).
